results.possibleIdentity: Ronald Rosenzweig (frequently spelled Ronald Rosensweig) is a prominent semiconductor engineer and entrepreneur who was active starting in the late 1950s. He graduated from the City College of New York (CCNY) in 1959 with a Bachelor's degree in Chemical Engineering. He began his career at the Radio Corporation of America (RCA), where he worked for approximately ten years, specializing in microwave transistors, semiconductor devices, and materials. While at RCA, he was part of the engineering community in the New York and New Jersey area and was a registered member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that promoted collaboration between engineers and artists. In 1968, he co-founded Microwave Semiconductor Corporation (MSC) in Somerset, New Jersey, which became a leader in the development of microwave power transistors and was eventually acquired by Siemens. In 1985, he co-founded ANADIGICS, Inc., a company focused on gallium arsenide (GaAs) integrated circuits, and served as its President and CEO for many years. Outside of his technical career, he and his wife Leslie are known as collectors of contemporary art.
